name: Philadelphia Pepper Pot
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the tripe the day before using. Wash thoroughly, place in a kettle and cover with water. Boil for 8 hours.
2. Remove the tripe when cooled, cutting into pieces about 1/2 inch square.
3. The next day, wash the veal knuckle and cover with 3 quarts of cold water. Simmer for about 3 hours, removing any scum that rises to the surface.
4. Remove meat from bones and cut into small pieces. Strain the broth and return it to the kettle.
5. Add the bay leaf, onion, and simmer for about 1 hour.
6. Add the potatoes (cut into squares) and pot herbs. Add the meat and tripe and season with salt and cayenne pepper if desired.
7. Make dumplings by combining the finely chopped suet, flour, salt, and enough water to allow rolling the dough into dumplings about the size of marbles. Flour well to prevent sticking and drop into the hot soup.
8. Cook for 10 minutes, add some chopped parsley, and serve immediately.
